name: Ice Age
subtitle: 2.6 Million – 11,700 Years Ago
description: A frozen world of breathtaking desolation. Colossal glaciers blanketed the northern continents, while sea levels plummeted. In this harsh theatre, our ancestors hunted mammoths and created the first symbolic art in deep caves. Fire was not merely warmth; it was civilisation's first heartbeat, allowing humanity to survive and dream amidst the ice.

milestones.title: Pleistocene Begins
milestones.desc: Earth enters the Ice Age cycle of glacial advances and retreats.

milestones.title: Fire Mastered
milestones.desc: Early humans gain control over fire, reshaping diet and social bonding.

milestones.title: Cave Art Emerges
milestones.desc: Abstract ochre drawings mark the first known symbolic human thought.

milestones.title: Tool Complexity
milestones.desc: Bone needles, fishhooks, and blade tools mark a cognitive revolution.

milestones.title: The Thaw
milestones.desc: Glaciers retreat, sea levels rise, and the Holocene epoch begins.

name: Ancient Era
subtitle: 10,000 BCE – 500 CE
description: From the fertile river valleys, humanity transformed from wandering hunters to settled citizens. The first seeds planted grew into law, art, and empire. From Sumerian script to Roman engineering, ancient civilisations built the foundations of the modern world. It was a vast, interconnected age of philosophy, trade, and monumental ambition.

milestones.title: Agricultural Revolution
milestones.desc: Humans settle in villages and begin farming in the Fertile Crescent.

milestones.title: Invention of Writing
milestones.desc: Sumerian cuneiform script enables record-keeping and literature.

milestones.title: Great Pyramid Built
milestones.desc: Egyptians erect the Great Pyramid of Giza — still the most massive stone structure on Earth.

milestones.title: Classical Philosophy
milestones.desc: Socrates, Confucius, and Buddha reshape moral and intellectual thought.

milestones.title: Fall of the Republic
milestones.desc: Julius Caesar is assassinated; Rome transforms into a global empire.

name: Industrial Era
subtitle: 1760 – 1900 CE
description: The roar of steam and the screech of iron rails marked the birth of the Industrial Age. Machines multiplied human output, pulling millions into growing cities. While Edison lit the night and Darwin reshaped science, the soot choked skies became nature's first invoice for progress; a debt we are still paying today.

milestones.title: Steam Engine
milestones.desc: James Watt perfects the steam engine, launching the industrial age.

milestones.title: First Railway
milestones.desc: Liverpool to Manchester railway opens — the world's first passenger rail line.

milestones.title: Origin of Species
milestones.desc: Darwin publishes evolution by natural selection, reshaping science and faith.

milestones.title: Electric Light
milestones.desc: Edison demonstrates the first practical incandescent light bulb.

milestones.title: First Flight
milestones.desc: The Wright Brothers achieve 12 seconds of powered flight at Kitty Hawk.

decision.title: ⚙️ Industrial Crossroads
decision.text: Your factory empire is booming. How do you power the next decade of growth?

choices.impact: Maximises output now but poisons rivers and skies. Future pollution +35.
Slower growth, cleaner rivers. Future pollution −15.

name: Modern Era
subtitle: 1900 – Present
description: From the Moon landing to the internet, the Modern Era is a compressed revolution of breathtaking change. We decoded DNA and placed supercomputers in every pocket, yet our progress has pushed the planet to a fever pitch. Humanity is now simultaneously more powerful and more vulnerable than ever before.

milestones.title: Nuclear Age
milestones.desc: The first atomic bomb detonates — humanity holds a power once only nature possessed.

milestones.title: Moon Landing
milestones.desc: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon. 600 million watch live.

milestones.title: World Wide Web
milestones.desc: Tim Berners-Lee publishes the first website, beginning the information age.

milestones.title: Smartphone Era
milestones.desc: The iPhone launches — a supercomputer in every pocket.

milestones.title: AI Inflexion
milestones.desc: Generative AI reaches 100 million users in 60 days, reshaping knowledge work.

decision.title: 🌐 Modern Policy Choice
decision.text: A landmark international summit gives your nation a binding vote. What is your priority?

choices.impact: Massive tech leap but energy hunger spikes. Future tech +40, pollution +10.
Slower tech growth but a healing planet. Future tech −10, pollution −40.

name: Future Era
subtitle: 2050 – 2150 CE
description: The future is not a destination, but a choice. It may be a world of clean fusion and eco cities, or one of rising seas and resource scarcity. The story of tomorrow is not yet written; it begins with the decisions we make in our boardrooms and living rooms today.

milestones.title: EV Tipping Point
milestones.desc: Electric vehicles surpass combustion cars globally for the first time.

milestones.title: Net Zero Target
milestones.desc: Nations' pledged deadline to reach net-zero carbon emissions.

milestones.title: Fusion Energy
milestones.desc: Commercial fusion reactors come online, delivering unlimited clean energy.

milestones.title: Climate Reckoning
milestones.desc: Earth's temperature trajectory locks in — 1.5°C or 4°C scenarios diverge.

milestones.title: Interplanetary Civilisation
milestones.desc: Permanent human settlements established on Mars and the Moon.

utopia.title: 🌿 Ecological Utopia
utopia.desc: Fusion energy powers a zero-waste civilisation. Rewilded forests cover half the land. Global temps stabilise at +1.2°C. Humanity flourishes in harmony with nature, reaching the stars.

techUtopia.title: 🤖 Technological Nirvana
techUtopia.desc: AGI solves climate, disease, and poverty simultaneously. Nanobot swarms purify oceans. Humans live to 150. The pace of change is breathtaking but inequality remains.

balanced.title: ⚖️ Cautious Progress
balanced.desc: Nations cooperate imperfectly. Temps rise +2.1°C before stabilising. Coastal cities adapt with sea walls. Technology lifts billions out of poverty, though nature remains threatened.

dystopia.title: ☠️ Climate Collapse
dystopia.desc: Carbon emissions spiralled past 550 ppm. +4°C scorched fertile zones. Wars over water and food destabilise nations. Two billion people become climate refugees by 2100.
